title: Gymnanthemum auriculiferum
taxonomy: Gymnanthemum auriculiferum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is a member of the tribe Anthemideae, a group of plants that are often referred to as daisy-like or composite flowers. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and growth habit. The genus Gymnanthemum is a relatively small group of plants, and Gymnanthemum auriculiferum is one of several species within this genus. Further classification of the plant at the level of family and genus is supported by molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro: Gymnanthemum auriculiferum, also known as East African wormwood, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnanthemum auriculiferum include essential oils, flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made with East African wormwood include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
